guest_role: Recurring panelists (VCs) on 20VC's weekly news-analysis roundtable
assertiveness_why: This is a panel-discussion format rather than a one-on-one interview, so Harry acts more as moderator/participant than interrogator, but he still injects data points, forces the panel to pick winners/losers, and occasionally challenges their framing ('Does that concern you to hear?').
question_style: Harry runs a rapid-fire, data-driven agenda (earnings numbers, stock moves) and repeatedly forces binary picks ('buy one and sell one'), functioning more as a moderator directing debate between Rory and Jason than as a traditional interviewer pressing a single guest.
tone: High-energy, irreverent, laced with profanity and inside jokes among three people who clearly know each other well; more like a trading-desk banter session than a formal interview.
depth: Extremely deep and technical on Mag-7 earnings, CapEx trends, token economics, AI ROI on engineering vs. other functions, SaaS company AI monetization — a specialist finance/tech-analyst level conversation.
harry_quotes: Does that concern you to hear?
What percent of CMOs can do that?
learnings: Panelists argue that hyperscaler AI revenue mostly reflects reselling LLM compute and tokens from OpenAI/Anthropic rather than owning defensible IP, except for Google which has its own frontier model.
Meta's AI CapEx lacks a clear revenue attribution story compared to Google's cloud backlog, which is why markets punished Meta's spending increase but rewarded Google's.

Palantir's enterprise moat comes from being one of few vendors that can credibly execute large, multi-million dollar AI transformation initiatives for Fortune 500 CEOs, unlike point-solution AI startups.

Real-world token spend for AI-driven marketing/customer-success agents came in far lower than expected (a few thousand dollars a month), casting doubt on some Anthropic-scale revenue projections outside of coding use cases.
Atlassian and Twilio's re-acceleration shows two different AI-monetization paths: upselling AI features to an existing base versus attracting entirely new customers via AI-driven demand.
notable: Not a standard guest interview — this is 20VC's recurring weekly news roundtable format with two regular VC panelists rather than a single spotlighted guest.
